meaning: n.冒险v.赌博,投机,孤注一掷
examples: It was wrong to gamble with our children's future.
Few firms will be willing to gamble on new products.
If you own shares in large American corporations, you're a winner in the weak-dollar gamble.
They would still not be allowed to gamble in the casinos aimed at tourists.
Experimenters have teased out this surprising conclusion by inviting subjects to gamble on the throw of dice.
